shelfmark: Bodl. MS heb. f 57/103 + Bodl. MS heb. f 57/102
description:
Letter fragment apparently from Alexandria to a notable called Yesod ha-Yeshiva, reporting that a woman named ʿAdhbiyya bt. Avraham (or possibly ʿArabiyya) stood security for her husband. The phrase 'tabarraʾaat min al-ketubba' appears. There are repeated references to "this great catastrophe" and various legal proceedings. (Information from Goitein's index cards). 

NB: the verso of Bodl. MS heb. f 57/102 is not digitized. 

The lower portion of the letter awaits transcription, and it's also not clear if this is actually a join (for one thing, the margin is much wider).
description_he: מכתב ביהודית-ערבית מאלכסנדריה. מוחזק בבודליאן אוקספורד.
